name: Alex, Mcomie
motto: A self made man? Yes—and worships his creator.—Henry Clapp
text: A self made man? Yes—and worships his creator.—Henry Clapp

name: Jos, Grue
motto: A woman needs a stronger head than her own for counsel—she should marry.—Calderon
clubs: A prominent figure in the drafting of the Student Body Constitution and instrumental in getting the Constitution printed
text: A woman needs a stronger head than her own for counsel—she should marry.—Calderon

name: A. B., Ballantyne
motto: He was so generally civil that no one thanked him for it.—Samuel Johnson
clubs: Member of Student Body Executive Committee
text: He was so generally civil that no one thanked him for it.—Samuel Johnson

name: Stewart, Jas. H.
motto: Your hair had ever an excellent color, but the curl, Ah! therein the beauty lies.—Anon.
text: Your hair had ever an excellent color, but the curl, Ah! therein the beauty lies.—Anon.

name: Peterson, Ras.
motto: I'm a fool, I know it; yet heaven help me, I'm poor enough to be a wit.
text: I'm a fool, I know it; yet heaven help me, I'm poor enough to be a wit.

name: Leigh, Jennie
motto: She is a woman, therefore may be wooed, She is a woman, therefore may be won.
text: She is a woman, therefore may be wooed, She is a woman, therefore may be won.

name: Pence, J. D.
motto: "The village all declared how much he knew; 'Twas certain he could write and cipher, too." —Oliver Goldsmith
text: "The village all declared how much he knew; 'Twas certain he could write and cipher, too." —Oliver Goldsmith

name: Dudley, Florence
motto: "For man's a giddy thing, and this is my conclusion." —Anon.
text: "For man's a giddy thing, and this is my conclusion." —Anon.

name: Hirst, C. T.
motto: "My life is one dem'd horrid grind." —Dickens.
text: "My life is one dem'd horrid grind." —Dickens.

name: Maughan, Inez
motto: "Who climbs the grammar tree, distinctly knows, Where noun, and verb and participle grows." —Dryden.
text: "Who climbs the grammar tree, distinctly knows, Where noun, and verb and participle grows." —Dryden.

name: Saxer, A. H.
motto: It matters not what men assume to be; Or good or bad, they are but what they are.—Bailey.
text: It matters not what men assume to be; Or good or bad, they are but what they are.—Bailey.

name: Kewley, Alice
motto: A child of our grandmother Eve, a female; or, for thy more sweet understanding, a woman.—Shakespeare.
text: A child of our grandmother Eve, a female; or, for thy more sweet understanding, a woman.—Shakespeare.

name: Oldham, W. B.
motto: "You look wise; pray correct the error."—Charles Lamb.
text: "You look wise; pray correct the error."—Charles Lamb.

name: Sonne, Nora
motto: "Distinguish our bondage as we will, 'Tis woman, woman rules us still."
text: "Distinguish our bondage as we will, 'Tis woman, woman rules us still."

name: Stewart, R. H.
motto: "I see and approve better things; I follow the worse."—Ovid.
text: "I see and approve better things; I follow the worse."—Ovid.

name: Frank, Wyatt
motto: Retire within thyself and thou wilt discover how small a stock is there. —Perseus.
text: Retire within thyself and thou wilt discover how small a stock is there. —Perseus.

name: Hendricks, Odessia
motto: A good woman is a treasure. —Saadi.
text: A good woman is a treasure. —Saadi.

name: Lee, O. L.
motto: His hair is of good color; an excellent color.—Shakespeare.
text: His hair is of good color; an excellent color.—Shakespeare.

name: Perry, Susannah
motto: A little, tiny, pretty, witty, charming, darling she.—Lucretius.
text: A little, tiny, pretty, witty, charming, darling she.—Lucretius.

name: Barrett, Elmer
motto: There is no substitute for thorough going, ardent, sincere, earnestness.—Dickens.
text: There is no substitute for thorough going, ardent, sincere, earnestness.—Dickens.

name: Curtis, R. B.
motto: 'Tis beauty that doth make women proud; 'Tis virtue that doth make them most admired.—Shakespeare.
description: A truly virtuous man is he who prides himself upon nothing.
text:
'Tis beauty that doth make women proud; 'Tis virtue that doth make them most admired.—Shakespeare.
A truly virtuous man is he who prides himself upon nothing.

name: Maughan, Venie
motto: 'Tis beauty that doth make women proud; 'Tis virtue that doth make them most admired.—Shakespeare.
text: 'Tis beauty that doth make women proud; 'Tis virtue that doth make them most admired.—Shakespeare.

name: Aaron, Rasmussen
motto: "Thou wilt scarce be a man before thy mother." —Beaumont and Fletcher
text: "Thou wilt scarce be a man before thy mother." —Beaumont and Fletcher

name: Ethel, Bennion
motto: "There is little of the melancholy element in her." —Anon
text: "There is little of the melancholy element in her." —Anon

name: E. F., Burton
motto: "His corn and cattle were his only care, And his supreme delight a country fair." —Dryden
clubs: R. F. A. Delta Theta Sigma
text: "His corn and cattle were his only care, And his supreme delight a country fair." —Dryden

name: Bullen, Asa
motto: "A man of mark." —Longfellow
text: "A man of mark." —Longfellow

name: Riter, Corlette
motto: "Man wants but little here below, Nor wants that little long." —Goldsmith
text: "Man wants but little here below, Nor wants that little long." —Goldsmith

name: Anderson, Carl
motto: "A minister but still a man."
text: "A minister but still a man."

name: Smith, W. R.
description: Sings in the choir. Keeps Thatcher guessing whether he is a nightingale or a canary. He has a Nightingale voice but canary legs.
text: Sings in the choir. Keeps Thatcher guessing whether he is a nightingale or a canary. He has a Nightingale voice but canary legs.

name: Ball, W. M.
description: Carries himself as a tin soldier should. Is not over fond of work.
text: Carries himself as a tin soldier should. Is not over fond of work.

name: Stratford, A. E.
description: Acknowledges in "Caruso" a possible rival as a tenor singer. Always telling what he is going to do.
text: Acknowledges in "Caruso" a possible rival as a tenor singer. Always telling what he is going to do.

name: Alder, Byron
description: Asserts that a head is a place for ideas; not for hair to loaf around on. Quiet and unassuming. Elected class president upon merit.
text: Asserts that a head is a place for ideas; not for hair to loaf around on. Quiet and unassuming. Elected class president upon merit.

name: Ivan, Egbert.
description: Not so slow as he looks. Great on "chasing up." Believes in Providence.
text: Not so slow as he looks. Great on "chasing up." Believes in Providence.

name: Elizabeth, V. Frazee.
description: She will make some home happy. Is an expert in the art of candy-making.
text: She will make some home happy. Is an expert in the art of candy-making.

name: June, Andrews.
description: Never bothers the girls. Only a moderate knocker. Always willing to work.
text: Never bothers the girls. Only a moderate knocker. Always willing to work.

name: Maggie, Costley.
description: Very discreet. Looks like she could be jolly if she wanted to.
text: Very discreet. Looks like she could be jolly if she wanted to.

name: M. O., Maughan.
description: Likes Logan, but lives at Wellsville to keep up the reputation of the town.
text: Likes Logan, but lives at Wellsville to keep up the reputation of the town.

name: Leroy, Beagley.
description: Quiet and unassuming. Believes in getting all that is possible out of school without telling anybody about it.
text: Quiet and unassuming. Believes in getting all that is possible out of school without telling anybody about it.

name: Peterson, J. L.
description: Is happy at present because he is learning how to run an auto. Avoids the girls for no good reason.
text: Is happy at present because he is learning how to run an auto. Avoids the girls for no good reason.

name: Hancock, Heber
description: He would impress you as a person of importance, but do not be deceived. Generally well liked by the girls. Is this a recommendation?
text: He would impress you as a person of importance, but do not be deceived. Generally well liked by the girls. Is this a recommendation?

name: Carlyon, Elizabeth
description: She always does the right thing at the right time.
text: She always does the right thing at the right time.

name: Robinson, Earl
description: Spends his time either on football field or at Kiester's Tailoring College. Slow and white headed, still not a Swede.
text: Spends his time either on football field or at Kiester's Tailoring College. Slow and white headed, still not a Swede.

name: Hendricks, Jessie L.
description: Has an imperious bearing. Makes the reader of popular novels believe she is a princess in disguise. She isn't, however. Lucille's only rival for "queening" honors.
text: Has an imperious bearing. Makes the reader of popular novels believe she is a princess in disguise. She isn't, however. Lucille's only rival for "queening" honors.

name: Knapp, A. J.
description: Plays the "Lunatic" to perfection. Although a married man, he is still happy.
text: Plays the "Lunatic" to perfection. Although a married man, he is still happy.

name: Charles-Leo, Merrill
description: His mother thinks he is a good boy. Well, let's not disillusion her.
text: His mother thinks he is a good boy. Well, let's not disillusion her.

name: Robert, Wrigley
description: Expects a permanent position as football manager. Has discovered a new Ester, the beginning of a series.
text: Expects a permanent position as football manager. Has discovered a new Ester, the beginning of a series.

name: J. W., Sessions
description: It pleases him to "argue the case." Keeps things on the move all the time and is an antidote for quietude.
text: It pleases him to "argue the case." Keeps things on the move all the time and is an antidote for quietude.

name: Arthur, Johnson
description: Pretty good looking when he wears his red tie and tan shoes. Very considerate of the girls. (He never bothers them.)
text: Pretty good looking when he wears his red tie and tan shoes. Very considerate of the girls. (He never bothers them.)

name: Sterling, Nelson
motto: "All that glitters is not gold," but all sterling is good silver.
description: This is a "Sterling" Nelson.
text:
"All that glitters is not gold," but all sterling is good silver.
This is a "Sterling" Nelson.

name: Robinson, D. E.
description: An editor of nature's designing. Acquired characteristic—"queening." Noted for his length. A good fellow.
text: An editor of nature's designing. Acquired characteristic—"queening." Noted for his length. A good fellow.

name: Nebeker, Pheobe
description: Never happy with less than ten "on the string," and she generally has them.
text: Never happy with less than ten "on the string," and she generally has them.

name: Brossard, Edgar
description: He has the size and the push. Getting rather fat and lazy now, though. Takes life as a huge joke, but has his serious moments. Our athletic hero.
text: He has the size and the push. Getting rather fat and lazy now, though. Takes life as a huge joke, but has his serious moments. Our athletic hero.

name: Bowman, A. E.
description: Doesn't believe in swearing. Has a good voice, curly hair, and an elongated figure. He isn't married yet.
text: Doesn't believe in swearing. Has a good voice, curly hair, and an elongated figure. He isn't married yet.

name: Olsen, J. K.
description: Cut out queening until his Junior year, but is now making it up.
text: Cut out queening until his Junior year, but is now making it up.

name: Plant, H. T.
description: Remarkable for doing unexpected things. Often acts rational.
text: Remarkable for doing unexpected things. Often acts rational.

name: Havenor, Eldora
description: Smiles a specialty, advises every one to work cheerfully, and practices what she preaches.
text: Smiles a specialty, advises every one to work cheerfully, and practices what she preaches.

name: Cook, L. L.
motto: "We may live without friends, we may live without books; But civilized man cannot live without Cooks."
description: Has acquired fame as a basketball player.
text:
"We may live without friends, we may live without books; But civilized man cannot live without Cooks."
Has acquired fame as a basketball player.

name: Gurjar, A. M.
description: A young man from hot Hindu land. In attempting his brain to expand Came to the A. C. "To study," said he, "How to irrigate India sand."
text: A young man from hot Hindu land. In attempting his brain to expand Came to the A. C. "To study," said he, "How to irrigate India sand."

name: Nelson, A. L.
description: Moves like a snail, thinks before he speaks, considers co-education a farce.
text: Moves like a snail, thinks before he speaks, considers co-education a farce.

name: Quayle, W. L.
motto: "Student activities too simple for me."
description: Who takes choir to get out of drill and astronomy to get out of choir.
text:
"Student activities too simple for me."
Who takes choir to get out of drill and astronomy to get out of choir.

name: Hendricks, Edith
description: Believes in love. In other words, she has never been disappointed.
text: Believes in love. In other words, she has never been disappointed.

name: Lambert, J. C.
description: Acts towards French as a resistance coil does towards electricity. Rather hard for the French to circulate, but when it does it warms him up and he retains the heat.
text: Acts towards French as a resistance coil does towards electricity. Rather hard for the French to circulate, but when it does it warms him up and he retains the heat.

name: Willey, J. A.
description: Has perverted the old axiom to "The mind of Willey grinds slow, but it grinds exceedingly fine." Carries a grouch continually and looks it.
text: Has perverted the old axiom to "The mind of Willey grinds slow, but it grinds exceedingly fine." Carries a grouch continually and looks it.

name: Cooley, A. C.
description: Diversified queening during his Freshman year, but has arrived at the green bag stage and queens only our fair associate editor.
text: Diversified queening during his Freshman year, but has arrived at the green bag stage and queens only our fair associate editor.

name: Jensen, Lucile
description: Likes cool things and takes things cool. Has many good qualities and but one fault. Wastes her queening accomplishments upon a single man. (Prof. Larsen—"in this case Single means one and not unmarried.")
text: Likes cool things and takes things cool. Has many good qualities and but one fault. Wastes her queening accomplishments upon a single man. (Prof. Larsen—"in this case Single means one and not unmarried.")

name: Ralph, E. T.
description: Goes around singing "Thursday Always Was My Jonah Day." He is the Thursday of a week without a Sunday. At present he has the enmity of the whole school because he writes Student Life Locals.
text: Goes around singing "Thursday Always Was My Jonah Day." He is the Thursday of a week without a Sunday. At present he has the enmity of the whole school because he writes Student Life Locals.

name: Snow, Chas.
description: Any fool can ask questions. So can Charles. Expects to discover a new enzyme.
text: Any fool can ask questions. So can Charles. Expects to discover a new enzyme.

name: Jones, W. L.
description: Next to Batt the most important man around the College. Cute little fellow with military bearing.
text: Next to Batt the most important man around the College. Cute little fellow with military bearing.

name: Busby, C. G.
description: Firm believer in luck, therefore grafts scions on dead trees.
text: Firm believer in luck, therefore grafts scions on dead trees.

name: Rich, Jannita
description: Labors under a disadvantage inasmuch as she came from Blackfoot.
text: Labors under a disadvantage inasmuch as she came from Blackfoot.

name: Wooley, V. C.
description: Is a quiet sort of a fellow with dark hair, but not an athlete. Not much for society. A scholar and a gentleman.
text: Is a quiet sort of a fellow with dark hair, but not an athlete. Not much for society. A scholar and a gentleman.

name: Walker, Genevieve Peterson
description: She has a sweet face and a sweet disposition which accounts for her being married. She is married and hence unattainable, which accounts for her sweetness.
text: She has a sweet face and a sweet disposition which accounts for her being married. She is married and hence unattainable, which accounts for her sweetness.

name: Newell, Comish.
description: Lives, breathes and dreams economics. Can tell all about the three economic stages of man.
text: Lives, breathes and dreams economics. Can tell all about the three economic stages of man.

name: Zundel, Geo. L.
description: Specialty, Botany. Favorite flower, "May Bell."
text: Specialty, Botany. Favorite flower, "May Bell."

name: Christensen, Annie C.
description: Believes that Salt Lake and not Boston is the "Hub of the Universe." Her religion is work. Smiles sometimes.
text: Believes that Salt Lake and not Boston is the "Hub of the Universe." Her religion is work. Smiles sometimes.

name: Holden, James
description: From the south in Gay Mexico, Come this young man we all know. At football a shark, Plays it in the dark, But alas! he forgot how to grow.
text: From the south in Gay Mexico, Come this young man we all know. At football a shark, Plays it in the dark, But alas! he forgot how to grow.

name: Christensen, Samuel
description: Has most beautiful white hair. Is rather superstitious. Believes it is his hair that enables him to play baseball. He has made but one mistake in life. He's married.
text: Has most beautiful white hair. Is rather superstitious. Believes it is his hair that enables him to play baseball. He has made but one mistake in life. He's married.

name: Brown, F. M.
description: Carries a smile always to go with his expanded sense of humor. A jolly good fellow to have around. Good Bluffer.
text: Carries a smile always to go with his expanded sense of humor. A jolly good fellow to have around. Good Bluffer.

name: Welch, J. S.
description: The "A. C." as an Agricultural School can boast of many "jacks," but this one is a "top-notcher" at braying.
text: The "A. C." as an Agricultural School can boast of many "jacks," but this one is a "top-notcher" at braying.

name: Winsor, L. M.
description: Feels happy when he has his hair curled and his uniform on. Of diversified fame. Annually breaks scores of maidens' hearts.
text: Feels happy when he has his hair curled and his uniform on. Of diversified fame. Annually breaks scores of maidens' hearts.

name: Paddock, J. S.
description: Although coming from Wisdom he was very considerate and left the town intact. A very well-behaved little boy—at times.
text: Although coming from Wisdom he was very considerate and left the town intact. A very well-behaved little boy—at times.

name: Froerer, Fred
description: A consummate politician. Manages to retain presidency of Ag. Club. Looks bright.
text: A consummate politician. Manages to retain presidency of Ag. Club. Looks bright.
